title: Bisect, Reproducible Diagnosis, and Automated Regression Search · Recovery Case: Gita Bisect Cause Verification
summary: A nondeterministic performance regression lies within 2,400 commits, skips unbuildable revisions, depends on generated assets, and may have entered on a merged side branch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita bisect cause verification increment in reproducible bisect harness and causal report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reproduce the boundary, inspect the candidate diff and dependencies, revert or patch experimentally, retain a regression test, and distinguish correlation from cause.
Handle untestable commits, flaky observations, merge topology, multiple introduction paths, and inconclusive adjacent candidates honestly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Bisect Cause Verification
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G78 is a theory in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Bisect Cause Verification contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G78 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe. A defensible solution applies Gita Bisect Cause Verification, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reproducible bisect harness and causal report.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 4
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set
git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts
# expected: bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity
# observation: the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID
# changed-case: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe
# transfer-limit: execute real historical code only in an isolated environment with bounded credentials and network
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The commit printed by bisect is automatically the root cause and its author owns the incident. This fails the Gita Bisect Cause Verification contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Build Gita Bisect Cause Verification evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-bisect-cause-verification-13v1un" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Bisect Cause Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-bisect-cause-verification-13v1un
# scenario-key: trace-13v1un probe-nu1v31 boundary-v1un13 owner-13v1unx fault-13v1unz
# before-ref: main=a11ce13 candidate=b00b1un
# hypothesis: the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set
git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts
# expected: bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity
# observation: the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID
# changed-case: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly
# after-ref: main=a11ce13 candidate=c0ffeen
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe
# transfer-limit: execute real historical code only in an isolated environment with bounded credentials and network
